claimText: Mpox is a viral zoonotic disease caused by the Monkeypox virus belonging to the Orthopoxvirus genus.
evidenceExcerpt: Mpox is a viral zoonotic disease caused by a virus of the Orthopoxvirus genus.
examUse: Statement-based question on the causative agent and nature of the disease.

claimText: Mpox is transmitted through close contact with infected entities.
evidenceExcerpt: It transmits through close contact with infected entities.
examUse: Statement-based question on the modes of transmission for viral diseases.

claimText: Diagnostic testing for Mpox is conducted using RT-PCR.
evidenceExcerpt: Diagnostic testing for Mpox is done using RT-PCR.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question on diagnostic tests for specific diseases.

claimText: There is currently no proven treatment for Mpox.
evidenceExcerpt: There is no proven treatment for Mpox.
examUse: Statement-based question regarding the availability of specific cures or treatments.
distractorHooks: Specific antiviral cure is widely available

claimText: Mpox was declared a Public Health Emergency of International Concern (PHEIC) by the WHO, but the outbreak in Africa is no longer considered a global health emergency.
evidenceExcerpt: It was declared a PHEIC by WHO, though the outbreak in Africa is no longer a global health emergency.
examUse: Statement-based question on the international health status and WHO declarations regarding the disease.
distractorHooks: Currently classified as a global pandemic
Never declared a PHEIC by WHO
Remains an active global health emergency
